name: Tic Tac Toe
description: Classic 3x3 grid game. Get three in a row to win!
rules:
OVERVIEW
Two players alternate placing marks on a 3x3 grid. First to get three in a row (row, column, or diagonal) wins. If the board fills with no winner, it's a draw.

WHAT YOU SEE
- board: array of 9 cells (null = empty, 'X' or 'O')
- currentPlayer: ID of the player whose turn it is
- marks: maps your player ID to 'X' or 'O'
- winner: player ID of winner (null if ongoing)
- draw: true if game ended in a draw

PHASE — PLAYING
You have actions only when currentPlayer equals your ID. legalActions lists the available moves — each is {type:'place', cell:N} for an empty cell (0=top-left, 8=bottom-right, row-major order). Copy one exactly from legalActions and submit it. If legalActions is empty, it is NOT your turn — poll and wait.

STRATEGY
1. Take the center (cell 4) on your first move if available — it's part of 4 winning lines.
2. Block your opponent's two-in-a-row immediately; winning > blocking only if you can win this turn.
3. Create a fork (two simultaneous threats) to force a win.

EMOTIONAL CUES
Winning feels clean and obvious. A draw against a strong opponent is respectable. Losing to a fork means you missed a double-threat setup.
